english: I'm starving. (lit: I have a wolf's hunger)
What are you up to tonight?
Are you coming with us tonight?
I can't wait. (lit: I don't see the hour)
Can you turn the music down?
I absolutely have to talk to you.
I was just about to call you.
Can you give me a hand?
Let's be straight with each other.
Do you understand what I mean?
What do you think about it?
What time are you coming back?
Wait, let me think about it.

italian: Hanno appena aperto un nuovo locale.
pronunciation: AN-noh ap-PEH-nah ah-PER-toh oon NWOH-voh loh-KAH-leh
english: They just opened a new place.

italian: Vivo a Milano da tre anni.
pronunciation: VEE-voh ah mee-LAH-noh dah TREH AN-nee
english: I've been living in Milan for three years.
I don't remember where I put it.
Can you explain it to me?
I can't make up my mind.

italian: Ho fatto una figura di merda.
pronunciation: OH FAT-toh OO-nah FEE-goo-rah dee MER-dah
english: I made a total fool of myself.
Have you seen the latest Netflix show?
Do you know where it is?

italian: Cosa vorresti fare il prossimo anno?
pronunciation: KOH-zah vor-RES-tee FAH-reh eel PROS-see-moh AN-noh
english: What would you like to do next year?
We need to find a solution.
I'll give you that. I agree with you on this.
What are you bringing to the party?
What did you think of it? (How did you find it?)
Shall we grab something to eat?
I drank more than I should have.

italian: Non riesco a dormire con questo caldo.
pronunciation: nohn RYES-koh ah dor-MEE-reh kon KWAY-stoh KAL-doh
english: I can't sleep in this heat.
I don't feel like going out.
I got back late last night.

italian: Finalmente si è aperto con me.
pronunciation: fee-nal-MEN-teh see EH ah-PER-toh kon meh
english: He/she finally opened up to me.

italian: Ho letto che stanno aprendo una nuova linea.
pronunciation: OH LET-toh keh STAN-noh ah-PREN-doh OO-nah NWOH-vah LEE-neh-ah
english: I read that they're opening a new line.
Remind me to call him tomorrow.

italian: Non riesco a smettere di pensarci.
pronunciation: nohn RYES-koh ah ZMET-teh-reh dee pen-SAR-chee
english: I can't stop thinking about it.
I don't know how to explain it.

italian: L'ho provato e non mi piace.
pronunciation: LOH proh-VAH-toh eh nohn mee PYAH-cheh
english: I tried it and I don't like it.
I chose the worst possible option.
I don't feel like doing anything.

italian: Non riesco a venire, mi dispiace.
pronunciation: nohn RYES-koh ah veh-NEE-reh, mee dees-PYAH-cheh
english: I can't make it, I'm sorry.
How do you say it in Italian?
You should have told me sooner.
I'm not having it. I disagree.

italian: Gli ho dato il mio numero.
Ci ho messo un po' a capirlo.
pronunciation: chee OH MES-soh oon POH ah kah-PEER-loh
english: It took me a while to get it.
I'm not so sure about that.

italian: Sto lavorando su qualcosa di nuovo.
pronunciation: stoh lah-voh-RAN-doh soo kwal-KOH-zah dee NWOH-voh

english: What do you feel like eating?
I was sleeping when you called.
I want to go back to Italy.
Call me if you need anything.
I'd like to get to know you better.
Open the door for me, please.

italian: Si è chiuso in se stesso.
pronunciation: see EH KYOO-zoh een seh STES-soh
english: He/she shut down and withdrew into themselves.

italian: Come si risponde a una cosa del genere?
pronunciation: KOH-meh see ree-SPON-deh ah OO-nah KOH-zah del jeh-NEH-reh
english: How do you respond to something like that?

italian: Non ho tempo di leggere ultimamente.
pronunciation: nohn OH TEM-poh dee led-JEH-reh ool-tee-mah-MEN-teh
english: I haven't had time to read lately.
Write to me when you get there.

italian: Non giocare con i miei sentimenti.
pronunciation: nohn joh-KAH-reh kon ee MYAY sen-tee-MEN-tee

english: I didn't know where to start.
Explain to me how it works.
It was exactly what I was looking for.
I need to talk to you.
What do you do for work?
Where are you going this weekend?
How did you come up with this idea?
I don't know how to say it.
I'd really love to visit Naples.
I still have to finish this work.
I've got it. I'll get this.
I thought you were in the loop.

italian: Ho bisogno di dormire almeno otto ore.
pronunciation: OH bee-ZON-yoh dee dor-MEE-reh al-MEH-noh OT-toh OH-reh
english: I need at least eight hours of sleep.
What time are you going out tonight?
I'll revisit it. I'll think about it again.
How much longer do we have to wait?
I called him but he's not answering.
We don't know each other yet.
Close that window, there's a draft.
I wrote a very long email.
When I was little I played football.
I used to live in a beautiful place.

italian: Cominciamo con il dire la verità.
pronunciation: koh-meen-CHYAH-moh kon eel DEE-reh lah veh-ree-TAH
english: Let's start by telling the truth.
You need to change your approach.

italian: Mi ha spiegato tutto nel dettaglio.
pronunciation: mee AH spyeh-GAH-toh TOOT-toh nel det-TAL-yoh

english: I had never tried it before.

italian: Devi scegliere da che parte stare.
pronunciation: DEH-vee SHEL-yeh-reh dah keh PAR-teh STAH-reh
english: You need to choose which side you're on.

italian: Ho smesso di fumare due anni fa.
pronunciation: OH ZMES-soh dee foo-MAH-reh DOO-eh AN-nee FAH
english: I quit smoking two years ago.
I didn't even know it existed.
Come on, don't be like that!
Come visit me whenever you want.
He/she doesn't help me at all.
She has changed for the better.
How does it make you feel?
It/he reminds me a lot of my father.
I found what I was looking for.
I can't keep going like this.
I need a change of scenery. (lit: I feel like changing air)
You have to see it to believe it.
Think about it and then tell me.
I can't find the right words.
I don't understand why you do it.
I won't lose sleep over it.
Let's get out of this situation.
I know people in that field.
Let's open up a serious conversation.
Let's wrap up here for today.
I only respond to people I know.

italian: Ho smesso di leggere i commenti.
pronunciation: OH ZMES-soh dee LED-jeh-reh ee kom-MEN-tee

english: You write the way you talk.

italian: Mi aiuta sapere che non sono solo.
pronunciation: mee ah-YOO-tah sah-PEH-reh keh nohn SOH-noh SOH-loh
english: It helps to know I'm not alone.

italian: Provaci, non hai niente da perdere.
pronunciation: PROH-vah-chee, nohn AH-ee NYEN-teh dah PER-deh-reh
english: Go for it, you have nothing to lose.
I always pick the worst moment.

italian: Non posso fare finta di niente.
pronunciation: nohn POS-soh FAH-reh FIN-tah dee NYEN-teh

italian: Non vedo dove vuoi andare a parare.
pronunciation: nohn VEH-doh DOH-veh VWOY an-DAH-reh ah pah-RAH-reh
english: I don't see where you're going with this.
I care about it a lot.

italian: Non me lo sarei mai aspettato.
pronunciation: nohn meh loh sah-RAY MAH-ee ah-spet-TAH-toh
english: I never would have expected it.
Do you have everything under control?
I'm out of here. I'm leaving.
You know what drives me crazy?
Do you want me to send it to you?
I need to be more careful.

italian: Non aspettare che le cose cambino da sole.
pronunciation: nohn ah-spet-TAH-reh keh leh KOH-zeh KAM-bee-noh dah SOH-leh
english: Don't wait for things to change on their own.
Let's turn a blind eye this time.
I didn't read all of it.
I'll write to you as soon as I can.

italian: Gioco sempre a fare il duro.
pronunciation: JOH-koh SEM-preh ah FAH-reh eel DOO-roh

italian: Non mi ricordo come si chiama.
pronunciation: nohn mee ree-KOR-doh KOH-meh see KYAH-mah
english: I don't remember what his/her name is.

italian: Non devi aiutarmi se non vuoi.
pronunciation: nohn DEH-vee ah-yoo-TAR-mee seh nohn VWOY
english: You don't have to help me if you don't want to.

italian: Non so come spiegartelo senza offenderti.
pronunciation: nohn soh KOH-meh spyeh-GAR-teh-loh SEN-tsah of-FEN-der-tee
english: I don't know how to explain it to you without offending you.

italian: Hai già deciso da che parte stare?
pronunciation: AH-ee JAH deh-CHEE-zoh dah keh PAR-teh STAH-reh
english: Have you already decided which side you're on?

italian: Scegli con la testa, non con il cuore.
pronunciation: SHEL-yee kon lah TES-tah, nohn kon eel KWOH-reh
english: Choose with your head, not your heart.
